campbell.title: Campbell City Council — May 5, 2026
campbell.summary: The Campbell City Council met to review financial investments managed by the Santa Clara Valley Transportation Authority for March 2026, adopted a new policy restricting city property use for federal civil enforcement activities, and discussed efforts to fill open city positions and improve employee retention.
keyTopics: Santa Clara Valley Transportation Authority investment report for March 2026
New city policy prohibiting federal civil enforcement on city-owned property
City job openings and strategies to recruit and retain employees

saratoga.title: Saratoga City Council — April 15, 2026
saratoga.summary: The city council held a public hearing to get feedback on plans to clean up hazardous conditions on several properties identified by the county, and voted to move forward with the cleanup work. The council also adopted a new technology policy and approved a process for residents to request accommodations if they need special access or services from the city.
keyTopics: Hazardous property cleanup ordered for county-identified sites
New city technology disruption policy adopted
Process established for residents to request reasonable accommodations

los-gatos.title: Los Gatos Town Council — May 5, 2026
los-gatos.summary: The Los Gatos Town Council met on May 5, 2026, to approve meeting minutes and handle several maintenance and service agreements. The council addressed hazardous vegetation as a public nuisance problem and approved reports for landscape and lighting work across town.
keyTopics: Declared hazardous brush and overgrown vegetation a public nuisance
Approved landscape and lighting maintenance engineer's report
Authorized multi-year service agreements with vendors (5-year contracts)
Approved minutes from previous April 21 meeting

san-jose.title: San José City Council — May 13, 2026
san-jose.summary: The San José City Council held a procedural meeting to review and finalize the agenda for their May 19 meeting, including adding new items and setting time limits for discussions. They also reviewed the meeting schedule for fiscal year 2026-2027, made appointments to various city committees and boards, and addressed a travel request from Council Member Foley.
keyTopics: May 19 City Council meeting agenda finalization
Fiscal Year 2026-2027 meeting schedule approval
City Council committee and board appointments
Council Member travel request

mountain-view.title: Mountain View City Council — May 14, 2026
mountain-view.summary: This is a notice about how Mountain View residents can participate in upcoming city council and board meetings. The city is reminding people how to submit questions in advance, where to find meeting agendas and details, and who to contact with questions.
keyTopics: Submit questions online before meetings at https://shorturl.at/TYgz9
Meeting agendas posted at least 24 hours in advance with specific locations
Contact City Manager's Office at 650-903-6301 or CNC@MountainView.gov with questions
Agendas and staff reports available at City Clerk's Office, 500 Castro Street, Third Floor

sunnyvale.title: Sunnyvale City Council — May 18, 2026
sunnyvale.summary: The Sunnyvale City Council met to approve previous meeting minutes and review grant applications from community groups seeking funding for local events and neighborhood projects this spring. Residents had the opportunity to speak publicly about these grant proposals.
keyTopics: Approval of December 2025 subcommittee meeting minutes
Spring 2026 Community Events and Neighborhood Grant Program applications under review
Public comment period for residents on proposed local grants

cupertino.title: Cupertino City Council — May 11, 2026
cupertino.summary: The City Council held an initial planning session to review Cupertino's proposed budget for fiscal year 2026-27. The meeting also covered requirements for lobbyists to register and report their activities if they try to influence city decisions.
keyTopics: FY 2026-27 budget proposal review and planning
Lobbyist registration and reporting requirements under city ordinance
Public testimony procedures for legislative and administrative matters

santa-clara.title: Santa Clara City Council — May 18, 2026
santa-clara.summary: Santa Clara's Neighborhood University Relations Committee is holding meetings that people can attend in person or join online via Zoom. The city is accepting written comments from residents before the meeting if they email by noon on meeting day.
keyTopics: Hybrid meeting format available (in-person and Zoom)
Zoom meeting link: https://santaclaraca.zoom.us/j/82990496428
Phone dial-in option: 1(669) 900-6833
Submit written public comments by emailing PlanningPublicComment@santaclaraca.gov by 12 p.m. on meeting day

milpitas.title: Milpitas City Council — May 5, 2026
milpitas.summary: The Milpitas City Council met to approve the city's five-year capital improvement plan for 2027-2031 and reviewed the next fiscal year's operating budget. The council also adopted a new master plan for parks and trails in the Innovation District and updated internal procedures for how council meetings are run.
keyTopics: 5-year capital improvement plan (2027-2031) approved
FY 2026-27 operating budget presentation
Innovation District Parks and Trails Master Plan adopted
Updates to City Council meeting procedures and handbook

palo-alto.title: Palo Alto City Council — May 15, 2026
palo-alto.summary: The City Council met to discuss plans for helping Palo Alto residents switch from gas to electric heating and appliances. The council reviewed proposed principles and programs to support this transition, which is needed to meet state air quality regulations.
keyTopics: Residential electrification programs and financial assistance options
Compliance with Air District regulations
Proposed principles to guide the city's electrification efforts
